events.title: Echoes of Liberty Exhibit
events.description: Echoes of Liberty, a collaborative exhibition between Arts Wayland and the Wayland Historical Society & Museum, opens in May in honor of America’s 250th anniversary. Artists were invited to create works inspired by ten historical artifacts connected to themes of liberty, freedom, and the pursuit of happiness. A public reception will be held on May 9 from 3–5 PM at the Arts Wayland Gallery (35 Andrew Ave, Wayland), where guest juror Christie Jackson, Curator and Director of Exhibitions at the Concord Museum, will select and announce ten artworks for display at the Museum during June and July. The exhibit may be viewed online at https://artswayland.com/collections/may-2026-art-exhibit-eol or in person at the gallery.

events.title: 2026 Vision Board Workshop
events.description: Reset Your Nervous System: An Adult Creative Journey Into 2026 Arts Wayland Gallery | 4 Thursdays beginning January 8 10:30–11:45 AM Designed for adults, this 4-week creative series offers a grounding, neuroscience-based approach to closing out 2025 and stepping intentionally into 2026. More than an art class or vision board workshop, this is a hands-on experience that helps you regulate your nervous system, reduce stress, and rewire your inner state through non-verbal creative expression. Using rhythm, bilateral movement, gratitude practices, and guided reflection, participants build a 2026 vision from a calm, aligned, and empowered place. No art experience required — just curiosity and a willingness to play. Register at: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Mindfulness and Art Workshop
events.description: Mindfulness & Art: Brain Rewired is a 6-week experiential series designed to cultivate presence, emotional regulation, and creative resilience. Led by instructor Isabel Zhang at Arts Wayland, this unique program invites participants to explore mindfulness through guided meditation, reflective prompts, and neurographic art—an intuitive, calming art practice that gently engages the brain and nervous system. No prior art experience is needed. Each session offers a supportive space to stay present with discomfort, build flexibility and self-trust, and move forward with greater intention—both on the page and in daily life. Thursdays, March 5–April 9 | 10:30–12:00 PM Arts Wayland Gallery, Wayland $333 for the 6-week series Register at www.artswayland.com

events.title: Zentangle Workshops
events.description: Zentangle with Nancy Longman: Beginner & Beyond the Basics Beginner Zentangle Sunday, March 15 | 1–3 PM Zentangle: Beyond the Basics Sunday, March 22 | 1–3 PM Location: AW Gallery Instructor: Nancy Longman Cost: $35 per class (materials included) Class Size: Minimum 5 / Maximum 16 students Discover the relaxing and creative Zentangle Method, an easy-to-learn approach to creating beautiful, structured patterns. In the Beginner Zentangle class, students will learn the eight original steps of the Zentangle Method, foundational tangles (patterns), and basic principles and vocabulary. Working on small paper tiles, you’ll create your own original piece while enjoying a calming, meditative drawing experience. No experience necessary. All materials are provided, and a beginner toolkit is yours to keep. In Zentangle: Beyond the Basics, students who have completed a beginner class will build on their skills by exploring shading techniques using graphite to add depth, contrast, and dimension. You’ll learn new tangles, experiment with different “strings,” and discover finishing touches that bring your artwork to life. Materials are provided; students with a beginner toolkit are encouraged to bring it. Register: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Teen Volunteering
events.description:
Registration 

Join us for our monthly teen volunteer sessions. Keep an eye on our social media for dates. 

These sessions are designed for teens ONLY - 12 to 18 year olds are welcome. If you are over 18 years and are looking for a volunteer opportunity, please email emily@natickfarm.org . 

Volunteer work at NCOF is primarily farm chore-based and outdoors. So whether you’re looking to be in a beautiful setting, or enjoy meeting new people, or want to learn the principles behind organic farming, come volunteer on the farm.

You must be pre-registered to volunteer at NCOF and must complete the Volunteer Registration Form which includes our volunteer agreement and waiver. 

A donation of $10 per person is suggested to help offset the costs associated with coordinating and planning these weekend volunteer sessions:  Donate here

events.title: Splash and Dash – Wayland Rev250 Celebration
events.description: May 30, 2026 • 8:30 AM The Splash and Dash is a community race that is being held as part of Wayland’s Rev250 Celebration marking the 250th birthday of the United States. The event begins at the Wayland Community Pool (WCP) for the “splash” and continues on the grounds of Wayland High School for the “dash”. Kids Race (12 & under) – 100 yard swim and 1/2 mile run Teen/Adult Race (13 & over) – 250 yard swim and 2 mile run Registration is $25 with pre-registration or $35 day-of. Rain or shine. All ages are welcome to join this fun event celebrating history, community, and local spirit!

events.title: American Revolution reenactment at Needham Town Common
events.description: For the third straight year, one of New England’s leading reenacting units, The King’s Own (4th Foot), is bringing its mix of history and fun to Needham Town Common May 30, 9 am-1 pm. See Redcoats drilling and preparing for war. Learn about equipment, organization, tactics, and weapons. Come face to face with soldiers sent to prevent or stop the American Revolution, and participate in an interactive debate on independence. Kids will have the chance to learn to march and handle (child safe!) muskets. Sponsored by the Needham Council for Arts and Culture.

events.title: King’s Own Come to Needham!
events.description: The British Are Coming! On Saturday, May 30th Needham will once again host The King’s Own, a group of reenactors who portray the British Redcoats who sought to prevent the American Revolution. Adults, teens, and children are invited to attend the fun, educational event that helps commemorate the 250th Anniversary of the founding of our country. See authentic uniforms, weapons, and drill, ask questions, and learn about the challenging days that led ordinary people of Needham into direct confrontation with their King and his army. Learn how British soldiers lived, fought, and made their way though seven years of warfare from Canada to the Caribbean. This event is sponsored by the Needham Cultural Council and is free to all.

events.title: King’s Own Come to Needham!
events.description: @ The King’s Own, a group of reenactors of the British Army in 1775, will once again return to Needham for a day of interactive learning. This year is the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence. See Redcoats at drill, learn what life was like in the 18th century, and dive into Needham’s history in an interactive event that should be fun and engaging for both adults and children. Sponsored by the Needham Culture and Arts Council. Add to calendar Google Calendar iCalendar Outlook 365 Outlook Live Details Date: Time:

events.title: Native Plant Speaker Series - Native Lawn Alternatives
events.description:
Registration 

The second in our lecture series aimed at connecting folks to native plants. 

David is the Senior Horticulturist at Garden in the Woods, a botanic garden in Framingham, MA devoted to the promotion and display of native New England plants. He has lived and worked as a garden professional in Massachusetts for over twenty years, having come for an internship at the Arnold Arboretum. His interest in native plants began while studying birds and working with Vermont Audubon, where he had moved after completing a Master’s degree in philosophy from The New School University in New York city. He was born and raised in Brooklyn, NY.

Thanks to a grant from Mass Cultural Council, this lecture will be free to attend. We do ask that you reserve a ticket for each member from your party to ensure we have enough space.

events.title: Backyard Chicken Workshop
events.description:
Registration 

$35/person
Whether you have had chickens for years or got them a couple months ago, this class is for you! This late spring class will be run by Ryder, a chicken enthusiast who has had backyard chickens for over 5 years, and Meeghan, the livestock manager of the NCOF.

Having chickens is truly one of the most rewarding experiences out there, and by knowing what you’re doing, it can feel all the more rewarding. But it can be hard to figure out what information to follow, especially at first. Articles all say different things, and books use language that is hard to understand, but by talking to someone who has gone through it before, you’ll end up more confident then you could imagine.

Please plan to bring your questions with you to the class; we will tailor our discussion topics based on the interests of the students.

This workshop is intended for ages 16 and up; if you have someone below that age interested, please let us know.

events.title: Kids Art and Leadership Lab Ages 11-13
events.description: Title: Kids’ Art & Leadership Lab: From the Inside Out Ages: 11–13 Dates: Saturdays – Jan 31, Feb 7, Feb 28, Mar 7, Mar 14, Mar 21, Mar 28, Apr 4 Time: 10:30 AM – 12:00 PM Tuition: $550 Materials Fee: $25 Minimum Enrollment: 8–15 students Location: Arts Wayland Register: www.artswayland.com Description: What if your child could learn to see their life the way an artist does — with imagination, openness, and courage to experiment? Designed for ages 11–13, this 8-week creative leadership lab blends art-making, brain science, and social-emotional learning to nurture focus, empathy, and resilience. Students explore how the principles of art—perspective, patience, expression—mirror the skills that shape emotional intelligence and leadership. Projects include designing meaningful keepsakes, guided reflection, and studying how creative leaders like Picasso and Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. turned vision into change. Each student builds toward a personal “masterpiece” of artistic and self-expression. Parents receive weekly recaps and simple at-home practices to extend learning beyond the classroom.

events.title: Hunnewell Estate Historical Tour
events.description: Saturday, May 30, 2026, 9:45 AM-12:00 PM The Tollhouse Shop, 229 Washington Street, Wellesley, MA, 02481 Space is limited! We have 24 spaces available per tour. Tickets here. Experience the rich history and vibrant present of the Hunnewell Estate with the Wellesley Historical Society’s exclusive walking tour. This 90-minute journey delves into the estate’s unique horticultural collection, offering insights into both its past and present. With only 24 spaces available per tour, this intimate experience ensures a detailed exploration of the gardens. Please note that the tour includes uneven terrain and requires all participants to use the provided transportation. Proceeds from the tour will benefit the Society’s general operating fund, supporting our mission to preserve local history.

events.title: Free movie screening of “Turning Red” at TCAN
events.description: Movie Screening: May 30th, 11am, at TCAN In celebration of Mental Health Awareness Month, the Natick Health Department is hosting a free screening of Turning Red at the TCAN Cinema Room. Turning Red introduces Mei Lee, a confident, dorky 13-year-old torn between staying her mother’s dutiful daughter and the chaos of adolescence. Her protective, if not slightly overbearing mother, Ming, is never far from her daughter—an unfortunate reality for the teenager. And as if changes to her interests, relationships and body weren’t enough, whenever she gets too excited (which is practically ALWAYS), she “poofs” into a giant red panda! Visit: turningred.com Location: 14 Summer St, Natick, MA 01760 The Center For Arts In Natick @11:00AM Register Here: https://tcan.org/events/turning-red/

events.title: Ponding at the Farm
events.description:
Registration  

Bring the whole family! This activity is geared towards kids aged 3 and up

Join us at the Farm for a hands-on adventure at Davis Brook, where you’ll uncover the hidden world of freshwater critters living just beneath the surface. Equipped with nets, trays and magnifying glasses, participants will explore the stream and get an up-close look at fascinating macroinvertebrates. It’s a fun, interactive way to discover the tiny creatures that play a big role in our ecosystem!

Thanks to a grant from Mass Cultural Council, this event is free to attend, but registration is still required.

All high school students in MA are required to select a state or local issue and prepare an advocacy plan. At the Showcase, more than 100 eleventh grade students in 50 groups will share their research, materials, and positions in a science fair type setting. The public is encouraged to come and talk with the next generation of policy advocates about the issues important to them. Visit www.lwv-needham.org for details.

events.title: Dreamscape: FJW Dance Studio 10th Anniversary Adult Showcase
events.description: Celebrating its 10th year Anniversary, FJW Dance Studio presents: DREAMSCAPE – A Classical Chinese Dance Showcase! Have you ever wondered what it would look like if a Chinese painting of ancient times came to life? Here’s a chance to catch a glimpse. Please join us at this experience of Chinese classical dance, performed by adult students and China-trained professionals. From the wonder of first encounters, to dances that evoke mountains and rivers; from embodying entire worlds within oneself, to dreams descending into the human realm. Every performance carries the expression of adult dancers’ love for life, passion, and dreams.

events.description: Arts Wayland Gallery: Members Exhibit Now through March 28th The Arts Wayland Gallery presents its month-long Members Exhibit, featuring one to five artworks from each participating member artist. This showcase includes a dynamic mix of paintings, photography, sculpture, and mixed‑media pieces, offering a rich view into the diverse creative talents within the Arts Wayland community. events.title: Zentangle Workshops
events.description: Zentangle with Nancy Longman: Beginner & Beyond the Basics Beginner Zentangle Sunday, March 15 | 1–3 PM Zentangle: Beyond the Basics Sunday, March 22 | 1–3 PM Location: AW Gallery Instructor: Nancy Longman Cost: $35 per class (materials included) Class Size: Minimum 5 / Maximum 16 students Discover the relaxing and creative Zentangle Method, an easy-to-learn approach to creating beautiful, structured patterns. In the Beginner Zentangle class, students will learn the eight original steps of the Zentangle Method, foundational tangles (patterns), and basic principles and vocabulary. Working on small paper tiles, you’ll create your own original piece while enjoying a calming, meditative drawing experience. No experience necessary. All materials are provided, and a beginner toolkit is yours to keep. In Zentangle: Beyond the Basics, students who have completed a beginner class will build on their skills by exploring shading techniques using graphite to add depth, contrast, and dimension. You’ll learn new tangles, experiment with different “strings,” and discover finishing touches that bring your artwork to life. Materials are provided; students with a beginner toolkit are encouraged to bring it. Register: www.artswayland.com

events.description: Echoes of Liberty, a collaborative exhibition between Arts Wayland and the Wayland Historical Society & Museum, opens in May in honor of America’s 250th anniversary. Artists were invited to create works inspired by ten historical artifacts connected to themes of liberty, freedom, and the pursuit of happiness. A public reception will be held on May 9 from 3–5 PM at the Arts Wayland Gallery (35 Andrew Ave, Wayland), where guest juror Christie Jackson, Curator and Director of Exhibitions at the Concord Museum, will select and announce ten artworks for display at the Museum during June and July. The exhibit may be viewed online at https://artswayland.com/collections/may-2026-art-exhibit-eol or in person at the gallery.

events.title: Natick Garden Club Plant Sale
events.description: The Natick Garden Club is hosting its annual plant sale on Sunday, May 31 from 10am-2pm indoors at the Natick Community-Senior Center, 117 East Central St. The sale is the group’s major fundraiser for the year. They offer a variety of plants that have been proven to grow successfully in our sometimes challenging weather conditions. Most of the plants are perennials from members’ gardens, and are labeled for bloom time and season, height, spacing needed, and sun and moisture requirements. They promote native plants as well, meaning they are native to our area, and tolerant of drought conditions and excellent at providing habitat for good insects and birds. There will be a modest houseplant and seedling section. Raffle tickets for a variety of items will be available and you don’t need to be present to win. Stay in the loop by signing up for Natick Report‘s free weekday email newsletter

events.title: Jane Mecom: Widow to Witness to the Revolution
events.description: Learn about the American Revolution through the life of someone who was living on the fringes of society, struggling to make ends meet. Jane Mecom was born in Boston in 1712 and experienced the changing political climate while enduring turmoil in her personal life, including, living in poverty, her husband’s death, and the loss of her children. Through surviving letters, we know that she experienced these trials in tandem.

events.description: Craft with Middle School Girl Scouts Sunday, May 31, 4:30 – 5:30 pm 5th – 7th graders snack & craft Adult orientation at 5:15 pm RSVP or ask questions: gstroop73505@gmail.com Fun & Friendship for Middle School Girls Wellesley Girl Scouts for 6th–8th graders combines fun, friendship, and adventure with community service. Scouts may also choose to pursue their Silver Award, a girl-led “take action” project which improves their community. Our troop meets monthly on Sundays late afternoon with one additional weekend activity scheduled each month. Participation is flexible based on your scout’s interests and schedule; our members attend various schools.This year’s activities have included: Community Service: archery and ice skating for younger girls, holiday party for Wellesley senior residents, and collecting food for Wellesley food Pantry. Scouts earned their animal helper badge by learning about the animals Unity Farm Sanctuary and crafting toys for Buddy Dog and Stray Pets in Need. Outings: apple picking, night climbing for Halloween, skiing at Wachusett, and back yard winter camping. The troop attended International Women’s Day at Mass Bay. We enjoyed the penguins at New England Aquarium and cannolis at Bova’s Bakery. The troop also bonded at beach volleyball, an arcade escape room, and cheered the Boston Legacy women’s soccer team to victory at Gillette Stadium. We will finish the year with a trip to Canobie Lake Park funded by our cookie sales. To RSVP or ask questions, please email gstroop73505@gmail.com Wellesley Girl Scouts are also recruiting girls at all grade levels (K-12) to form new troops or join existing troops in the fall. If you’re interested in joining Girl Scouts, please contact wellesleygs@gmail.com or customercare@gsema.org

events.description: The Artist Page: A Creative Flow & Expression — A 6 Week Journey Creativity doesn’t come from trying harder — it comes from listening, softening, and allowing your voice to return. Join Isabel Zhang for The Artist Page, a six week guided creative practice designed to move beyond overthinking and into authentic expression. Each session includes grounding, gentle prompts for writing, drawing, or a blend of both, and optional sharing in a supportive, non evaluative space. Classes meet on Tuesdays from 10 to 11:30 AM on March 17, 24, 31 and April 7, 14, 28 (no session April 21) at Arts Wayland Gallery, 35 Andrew Ave, Wayland. Reclaim your creative flow and explore authentic expression — register now at www.artswayland.com .

events.title: 2026 Vision Board Workshop
events.description: Reset Your Nervous System: An Adult Creative Journey Into 2026 Arts Wayland Gallery | 4 Thursdays beginning January 8 10:30–11:45 AM Designed for adults, this 4-week creative series offers a grounding, neuroscience-based approach to closing out 2025 and stepping intentionally into 2026. More than an art class or vision board workshop, this is a hands-on experience that helps you regulate your nervous system, reduce stress, and rewire your inner state through non-verbal creative expression. Using rhythm, bilateral movement, gratitude practices, and guided reflection, participants build a 2026 vision from a calm, aligned, and empowered place. No art experience required — just curiosity and a willingness to play. Register at: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Mindfulness and Art Workshop
events.description: Mindfulness & Art: Brain Rewired is a 6-week experiential series designed to cultivate presence, emotional regulation, and creative resilience. Led by instructor Isabel Zhang at Arts Wayland, this unique program invites participants to explore mindfulness through guided meditation, reflective prompts, and neurographic art—an intuitive, calming art practice that gently engages the brain and nervous system. No prior art experience is needed. Each session offers a supportive space to stay present with discomfort, build flexibility and self-trust, and move forward with greater intention—both on the page and in daily life. Thursdays, March 5–April 9 | 10:30–12:00 PM Arts Wayland Gallery, Wayland $333 for the 6-week series Register at www.artswayland.com

events.title: 50 Hikes Through History
events.description: In Massachusetts, many parks and trails have been carved out of historical sites. Author Alison O’Leary, a journalist and coauthor of the Appalachian Mountain Club’s guidebook, Best Day Hikes Near Boston, shares her favorites through this hour-long program. Using maps and historic photos, O’Leary describes several day trip destinations with historical significance and interesting features. Special effort is made to include varying levels of difficulty (flat and easy to steep and challenging) and variety of scenery. June 4, 7pm, Wellesley Free Library main branch. Register online. events.title: Charles River Center, Lifeworks and Riverside to host legislative breakfast on June 5
events.description: Disability service providers Charles River Center, Lifeworks and Riverside Community Health will host a joint legislative breakfast beginning at 8:30 a.m. on Friday, June 5, 2026, at the at the Sheraton Boston Needham Hotel, 100 Cabot Street, Needham, MA. Senator Rebecca Rauch, Representative Paul McMurtry, Representative Edward Philips, and Representative Joshua Tarsky plan to attend the event. Each legislator will have the opportunity to speak during the program, and family advocates from Charles River, Lifeworks and Riverside will share their experiences with legislators. The event will run from 8:30 a.m. to 10:30 a.m. For more information or to register, visit www.charlesrivercenter.org/slide/legislative-breakfast-2/.

events.title: Zentangle Workshops
events.description: Zentangle with Nancy Longman: Beginner & Beyond the Basics Beginner Zentangle Sunday, March 15 | 1–3 PM Zentangle: Beyond the Basics Sunday, March 22 | 1–3 PM Location: AW Gallery Instructor: Nancy Longman Cost: $35 per class (materials included) Class Size: Minimum 5 / Maximum 16 students Discover the relaxing and creative Zentangle Method, an easy-to-learn approach to creating beautiful, structured patterns. In the Beginner Zentangle class, students will learn the eight original steps of the Zentangle Method, foundational tangles (patterns), and basic principles and vocabulary. Working on small paper tiles, you’ll create your own original piece while enjoying a calming, meditative drawing experience. No experience necessary. All materials are provided, and a beginner toolkit is yours to keep. In Zentangle: Beyond the Basics, students who have completed a beginner class will build on their skills by exploring shading techniques using graphite to add depth, contrast, and dimension. You’ll learn new tangles, experiment with different “strings,” and discover finishing touches that bring your artwork to life. Materials are provided; students with a beginner toolkit are encouraged to bring it. Register: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Kids Art and Leadership Lab Ages 11-13
events.description: Title: Kids’ Art & Leadership Lab: From the Inside Out Ages: 11–13 Dates: Saturdays – Jan 31, Feb 7, Feb 28, Mar 7, Mar 14, Mar 21, Mar 28, Apr 4 Time: 10:30 AM – 12:00 PM Tuition: $550 Materials Fee: $25 Minimum Enrollment: 8–15 students Location: Arts Wayland Register: www.artswayland.com Description: What if your child could learn to see their life the way an artist does — with imagination, openness, and courage to experiment? Designed for ages 11–13, this 8-week creative leadership lab blends art-making, brain science, and social-emotional learning to nurture focus, empathy, and resilience. Students explore how the principles of art—perspective, patience, expression—mirror the skills that shape emotional intelligence and leadership. Projects include designing meaningful keepsakes, guided reflection, and studying how creative leaders like Picasso and Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. turned vision into change. Each student builds toward a personal “masterpiece” of artistic and self-expression. Parents receive weekly recaps and simple at-home practices to extend learning beyond the classroom.

events.title: Wellesley Trails Committee Guided Walk—Mini-Grand Tour
events.description: Join the Wellesley Trails Committee on a guided trail walk. Free. No registration required. Walks take place unless it pours rain or there are high winds. Dogs on leashes are welcome. DATE: Saturday, June 6, 2026 TIME: 10:00am-12:30pm LOCATION: Meet at the Longfellow Pond parking lot at 220 Oakland Street DESCRIPTION: Mini-Grand Tour (6.5 miles) Join us for a 6.5-mile, 2.5+ hour Mini-Grand Tour that links a selection of our favorite trails, with a few sidewalks in between. From Longfellow Pond, we will find our way to Centennial Reservation, the Crosstown Trail at Indian Springs Park, John G. Schuler Path, Hunnewell Fyffe footbridge, Charles River Path, Oullet Park, and Barton Road trail, before returning to the Town Forest. While not as long as the Grand Tour, the Mini Tour is more strenuous than our typical walk. Remember to bring water and wear sturdy walking shoes. Meet at the Longfellow Pond parking lot at 220 Oakland Street.

events.title: Experiencing the Joy of Cycling: Free Adaptive Rail Trail Rides in Sudbury (June 6, August 22 & September 19)
events.description: Residents of Wayland and beyond of all ages and abilities are invited to experience the joy of cycling this summer and fall through Rail Trail Rides: Sudbury, a free adaptive cycling program designed to create inclusive outdoor recreation opportunities for people of all abilities. Hosted by the Town of Sudbury’s Commission on Disability and Rail Trails Advisory Committee in partnership with All Out Adventures, rides will take place on June 6, August 22, and September 19 at the Broadacres Farm Parking Lot (62 Morse Road, Sudbury, MA), with one-hour sessions offered between 11:00 a.m. and 3:00 p.m. Participants can choose from a variety of adaptive cycles, including recumbent trikes, handcycles, and tandem bikes, with trained staff on-site to ensure a safe, supportive experience. Helmets are provided, though riders may bring their own. “This program is about access, inclusion, and joy,” organizers shared. “We want everyone in our community to feel welcome and able to experience the rail trail in a way that works for them.” Sign Up to Ride Advance registration is required for one-hour sessions, and participants are encouraged to join for one or more rides throughout the season. Register online or call 413-584-2052: June 6 (11am–3pm): https://alloutadventures.app.neoncrm.com/np/clients/alloutadventures/event.jsp?event=15250& August 22 (11am–3pm): https://alloutadventures.app.neoncrm.com/np/clients/alloutadventures/event.jsp?event=15255& September 19 (11am–3pm): https://alloutadventures.app.neoncrm.com/np/clients/alloutadventures/event.jsp?event=15260& Whether you are an experienced cyclist or trying it for the first time, Rail Trail Rides offers a meaningful way to enjoy the outdoors in an inclusive, welcoming setting.

Each session includes grounding, gentle prompts for writing, drawing, or a blend of both, and optional sharing in a supportive, non evaluative space. Reclaim your creative flow and explore authentic expression — register now at www.artswayland.com .

events.title: Echoes of Liberty Exhibit
events.description: Echoes of Liberty, a collaborative exhibition between Arts Wayland and the Wayland Historical Society & Museum, opens in May in honor of America’s 250th anniversary. Artists were invited to create works inspired by ten historical artifacts connected to themes of liberty, freedom, and the pursuit of happiness. A public reception will be held on May 9 from 3–5 PM at the Arts Wayland Gallery (35 Andrew Ave, Wayland), where guest juror Christie Jackson, Curator and Director of Exhibitions at the Concord Museum, will select and announce ten artworks for display at the Museum during June and July. The exhibit may be viewed online at https://artswayland.com/collections/may-2026-art-exhibit-eol or in person at the gallery.

events.title: 2026 Vision Board Workshop
events.description: Reset Your Nervous System: An Adult Creative Journey Into 2026 Arts Wayland Gallery | 4 Thursdays beginning January 8 10:30–11:45 AM Designed for adults, this 4-week creative series offers a grounding, neuroscience-based approach to closing out 2025 and stepping intentionally into 2026. More than an art class or vision board workshop, this is a hands-on experience that helps you regulate your nervous system, reduce stress, and rewire your inner state through non-verbal creative expression. Using rhythm, bilateral movement, gratitude practices, and guided reflection, participants build a 2026 vision from a calm, aligned, and empowered place. No art experience required — just curiosity and a willingness to play. Register at: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Mindfulness and Art Workshop
events.description: Mindfulness & Art: Brain Rewired is a 6-week experiential series designed to cultivate presence, emotional regulation, and creative resilience. Led by instructor Isabel Zhang at Arts Wayland, this unique program invites participants to explore mindfulness through guided meditation, reflective prompts, and neurographic art—an intuitive, calming art practice that gently engages the brain and nervous system. No prior art experience is needed. Each session offers a supportive space to stay present with discomfort, build flexibility and self-trust, and move forward with greater intention—both on the page and in daily life. Thursdays, March 5–April 9 | 10:30–12:00 PM Arts Wayland Gallery, Wayland $333 for the 6-week series Register at www.artswayland.com

events.title: Author visit and luncheon, Wellesley Books—Julie Schumacher, “Patient, Female”
events.description: photo by Brice MacLaren EVENT: luncheon with Dear Committee Members author Julie Schumacher to celebrate the release of her new book, Patient, Female. DATE: Friday, June 12th, 2026 @ Noon This in-person event will take place at Wellesley Books, 82 Central Street, Wellesley, MA. $37.50 ticket includes a copy of the book and lunch. Buy tickets online with the link below (fees apply) or by calling 781-431-1160 during store hours (no fees). https://wellesleybooks.com/event/schumacher-luncheon DESCRIPTION: Exquisitely honest and expertly crafted, Patient, Female renders—with dark humor and wit—the foibles of human behavior and our endearing imperfections. Each protagonist—ranging from girlhood to senescence—receives her own indelible voice as she navigates social blunders, generational misunderstandings, and the absurdity of the human experience.

events.title: Zentangle Workshops
events.description: Zentangle with Nancy Longman: Beginner & Beyond the Basics Beginner Zentangle Sunday, March 15 | 1–3 PM Zentangle: Beyond the Basics Sunday, March 22 | 1–3 PM Location: AW Gallery Instructor: Nancy Longman Cost: $35 per class (materials included) Class Size: Minimum 5 / Maximum 16 students Discover the relaxing and creative Zentangle Method, an easy-to-learn approach to creating beautiful, structured patterns. In the Beginner Zentangle class, students will learn the eight original steps of the Zentangle Method, foundational tangles (patterns), and basic principles and vocabulary. Working on small paper tiles, you’ll create your own original piece while enjoying a calming, meditative drawing experience. No experience necessary. All materials are provided, and a beginner toolkit is yours to keep. In Zentangle: Beyond the Basics, students who have completed a beginner class will build on their skills by exploring shading techniques using graphite to add depth, contrast, and dimension. You’ll learn new tangles, experiment with different “strings,” and discover finishing touches that bring your artwork to life. Materials are provided; students with a beginner toolkit are encouraged to bring it. Register: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Kids Art and Leadership Lab Ages 11-13
events.description: Title: Kids’ Art & Leadership Lab: From the Inside Out Ages: 11–13 Dates: Saturdays – Jan 31, Feb 7, Feb 28, Mar 7, Mar 14, Mar 21, Mar 28, Apr 4 Time: 10:30 AM – 12:00 PM Tuition: $550 Materials Fee: $25 Minimum Enrollment: 8–15 students Location: Arts Wayland Register: www.artswayland.com Description: What if your child could learn to see their life the way an artist does — with imagination, openness, and courage to experiment? Designed for ages 11–13, this 8-week creative leadership lab blends art-making, brain science, and social-emotional learning to nurture focus, empathy, and resilience. Students explore how the principles of art—perspective, patience, expression—mirror the skills that shape emotional intelligence and leadership. Projects include designing meaningful keepsakes, guided reflection, and studying how creative leaders like Picasso and Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. turned vision into change. Each student builds toward a personal “masterpiece” of artistic and self-expression. Parents receive weekly recaps and simple at-home practices to extend learning beyond the classroom.

events.title: All Aboard, an accessible and adaptive cycling program on Cochituate Rail Trail
events.description: The Town of Natick presents All Aboard, an accessible and adaptive cycling program. Anyone that has a mobility limitation and wants to experience cycling is welcome and encouraged to participate. The Friends of Natick Trails supports the All Aboard program. To register as a rider, click the link below: June 13, 2026 Time slots: 10am-3pm Meeting spot: 11 Mechanic St., Natick, MA 01760 Natick is partnering with All Out Adventures, who brings a fleet of adaptive and accessible tricycles and bicycles to Natick on scheduled Saturdays. Staff will ensure a proper fit, and volunteers are available to accompany riders. All rides will start at the Mechanic Street lot on the Cochituate Rail Trail. This program is free to riders. Please note that advance registration is required for riders and volunteers.

Ask questions, give input, and share concerns about Town operations, economic development, housing, and other issues. Select Board office hours are open to everyone in the community. An appointment is not necessary. Can’t make Select Board office hours, but have questions? Email Board members at Selectboard@natickma.org

events.title: 251st U.S. Army Birthday Celebration in Natick
events.description: 251st U.S. Army Birthday Celebration June 13th | 10AM – 3PM On behalf of the U.S. Army Natick Soldier Systems Center, we would like to extend an invitation to your organization, members, families, and community partners to attend the 251st U.S. Army Birthday Celebration on Saturday, June 13 from 10 a.m. to 3 p.m. at Natick Soldier Systems Center in Natick at 10 General Greene Ave. This free, public event will bring together Soldiers, Veterans, families, and the surrounding communities for a day of celebration, connection, and Army pride. Guests will have the opportunity to experience: • A Technology Expo showcasing the future of Soldier systems and innovation • A Swearing-In Ceremony honoring our nation’s newest Soldiers • A Birthday Car Show featuring classic and modern vehicles • Family-friendly activities, games, inflatables, and food trucks • A Flag Day Ceremony led by local Cub Scouts • Special appearances and community engagement opportunities No RSVP Required Expand your reach (and support local news) by advertising on Natick Report

events.title: Wellesley Trails Committee Guided Walk—Centennial Reservation
events.description: Join the Wellesley Trails Committee on a guided trail walk. Free. No registration required. Walks take place unless it pours rain or there are high winds. Dogs on leashes are welcome. DATE: Saturday, June 13 TIME: 10am-11:15am LOCATION: Meet at the Mass Bay parking lot off of Oakland Street, (not the Centennial lot) DESCRIPTION: Centennial Reservation (1.8 miles). Take a picturesque walk, through rolling meadows to the top of Maugus Hill, one of the highest points in Wellesley. View the beautiful Great Blue Hills in Canton from a hillside meadow overlooking the town of Needham. Then, descend through the meadow and around Benzanson Pond, a favorite swimming hole for dogs as well as frogs and tadpoles. Come explore this conservation land purchased by the town for Wellesley’s 1981 Centennial Celebration and now one of the most popular trails in Wellesley. This walk also includes Mass Bay land that is maintained by the Wellesley Trails Committee and the Wellesley DPW.

location.venue: Mass Bay parking lot off of Oakland Street

events.title: Annual Farm Fun Run
events.description:
Registration 

Run, walk, saunter or mosey through the beautiful Natick Community Organic Farm and surrounding conservation land. Our scenic course is a 3 x 1 mile loop that includes road, gravel, grass, and trail. Farm or animal themed costume are encouraged but NOT required!

This event is for runners and walkers of all ages. Kids have the option of completing the full three laps (approximately 5k) or just one lap. All participants will receive a commemorative chicken medal!

Funds raised will go towards supporting programs at the Farm

events.title: PMC Kids Ride Newton-Needham
events.description: The PMC Kids Ride program is a spoke in the wheel of the Pan Mass Challenge’s yearly fundraising campaign, featuring mini-bike-a-thons for children ages 2-15 and providing them with the opportunity to fight cancer, just like the adults. On June 14, 2026, the PMC Kids Ride Newton-Needham will happen at Shark Ninja in Needham. Kids will ride routes to raise funds for pediatric cancer research and support at the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute. There will be an after party with snacks, a raffle, and more! For more information, to register to ride or volunteer to help, to donate to our ride, please visit our website: Kids.PMC.org/Newton-Needham Empower kids to make an impact in the fight against cancer! NOW MORE THAN EVER!

events.title: Weaving The Wrack Line
events.description: Weaving the Wrack Line with Rebecca McGee Tuck Join fiber artist and ocean activist Rebecca McGee Tuck for Weaving the Wrack Line, an interactive weaving workshop that transforms marine debris and reclaimed materials into meaningful works of art. As part of Arts Wayland’s Eco Month, participants will learn simple weaving techniques on frame looms, experiment with alternative materials, and create a small weaving that reflects both personal creativity and environmental stewardship. events.title: The Artist Page
events.description: The Artist Page: A Creative Flow & Expression — A 6 Week Journey Creativity doesn’t come from trying harder — it comes from listening, softening, and allowing your voice to return. Join Isabel Zhang for The Artist Page, a six week guided creative practice designed to move beyond overthinking and into authentic expression. Each session includes grounding, gentle prompts for writing, drawing, or a blend of both, and optional sharing in a supportive, non evaluative space. Classes meet on Tuesdays from 10 to 11:30 AM on March 17, 24, 31 and April 7, 14, 28 (no session April 21) at Arts Wayland Gallery, 35 Andrew Ave, Wayland. Reclaim your creative flow and explore authentic expression — register now at www.artswayland.com .

2026 Vision Board Workshop
events.description: Reset Your Nervous System: An Adult Creative Journey Into 2026 Arts Wayland Gallery | 4 Thursdays beginning January 8 10:30–11:45 AM Designed for adults, this 4-week creative series offers a grounding, neuroscience-based approach to closing out 2025 and stepping intentionally into 2026. More than an art class or vision board workshop, this is a hands-on experience that helps you regulate your nervous system, reduce stress, and rewire your inner state through non-verbal creative expression. Using rhythm, bilateral movement, gratitude practices, and guided reflection, participants build a 2026 vision from a calm, aligned, and empowered place. No art experience required — just curiosity and a willingness to play. Register at: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Mindfulness and Art Workshop
events.description: Mindfulness & Art: Brain Rewired is a 6-week experiential series designed to cultivate presence, emotional regulation, and creative resilience. Led by instructor Isabel Zhang at Arts Wayland, this unique program invites participants to explore mindfulness through guided meditation, reflective prompts, and neurographic art—an intuitive, calming art practice that gently engages the brain and nervous system. No prior art experience is needed. Each session offers a supportive space to stay present with discomfort, build flexibility and self-trust, and move forward with greater intention—both on the page and in daily life. Thursdays, March 5–April 9 | 10:30–12:00 PM Arts Wayland Gallery, Wayland $333 for the 6-week series Register at www.artswayland.com

events.title: Reading Frederick Douglass & Celebrating Juneteenth
events.description: The Natick Historical Society and Natick is United will co-host a community reading of Frederick Douglass’ 1852 speech, “What to a Slave is the 4th of July?” at 10:30 AM on Natick Common. Historian and Natick resident, Brenna Greer, will offer an introduction to the speech and facilitate dialogue afterwards. Local drummer Bruno Giles will kick off the event with drumming at 10am. If you would like to sign up to participate in this event by reading part of the speech (1-3 paragraphs) please sign up. Questions? Email director@natickhistoricalsociety.org The Juneteenth celebration continues on Saturday, June 20 with Natick Center Cultural District’s Juneteenth event on Natick Common from 11:00 AM– 1:00 PM. There will be food by Mig’s Dogs, fun activities, and musical performances by Drum Nomads. Expand your reach (and support local news) via paid advertisement on Natick Report

events.title: Zentangle Workshops
events.description: Zentangle with Nancy Longman: Beginner & Beyond the Basics Beginner Zentangle Sunday, March 15 | 1–3 PM Zentangle: Beyond the Basics Sunday, March 22 | 1–3 PM Location: AW Gallery Instructor: Nancy Longman Cost: $35 per class (materials included) Class Size: Minimum 5 / Maximum 16 students Discover the relaxing and creative Zentangle Method, an easy-to-learn approach to creating beautiful, structured patterns. In the Beginner Zentangle class, students will learn the eight original steps of the Zentangle Method, foundational tangles (patterns), and basic principles and vocabulary. Working on small paper tiles, you’ll create your own original piece while enjoying a calming, meditative drawing experience. No experience necessary. All materials are provided, and a beginner toolkit is yours to keep. In Zentangle: Beyond the Basics, students who have completed a beginner class will build on their skills by exploring shading techniques using graphite to add depth, contrast, and dimension. You’ll learn new tangles, experiment with different “strings,” and discover finishing touches that bring your artwork to life. Materials are provided; students with a beginner toolkit are encouraged to bring it. Register: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Kids Art and Leadership Lab Ages 11-13
events.description: Title: Kids’ Art & Leadership Lab: From the Inside Out Ages: 11–13 Dates: Saturdays – Jan 31, Feb 7, Feb 28, Mar 7, Mar 14, Mar 21, Mar 28, Apr 4 Time: 10:30 AM – 12:00 PM Tuition: $550 Materials Fee: $25 Minimum Enrollment: 8–15 students Location: Arts Wayland Register: www.artswayland.com Description: What if your child could learn to see their life the way an artist does — with imagination, openness, and courage to experiment? Designed for ages 11–13, this 8-week creative leadership lab blends art-making, brain science, and social-emotional learning to nurture focus, empathy, and resilience. Students explore how the principles of art—perspective, patience, expression—mirror the skills that shape emotional intelligence and leadership. Projects include designing meaningful keepsakes, guided reflection, and studying how creative leaders like Picasso and Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. turned vision into change. Each student builds toward a personal “masterpiece” of artistic and self-expression. Parents receive weekly recaps and simple at-home practices to extend learning beyond the classroom.

events.title: The Artist Page
events.description: The Artist Page: A Creative Flow & Expression — A 6 Week Journey Creativity doesn’t come from trying harder — it comes from listening, softening, and allowing your voice to return. Join Isabel Zhang for The Artist Page, a six week guided creative practice designed to move beyond overthinking and into authentic expression. Each session includes grounding, gentle prompts for writing, drawing, or a blend of both, and optional sharing in a supportive, non evaluative space. Classes meet on Tuesdays from 10 to 11:30 AM on March 17, 24, 31 and April 7, 14, 28 (no session April 21) at Arts Wayland Gallery, 35 Andrew Ave, Wayland. Reclaim your creative flow and explore authentic expression — register now at www.artswayland.com .

events.title: Zentangle Workshops
events.description: Zentangle with Nancy Longman: Beginner & Beyond the Basics Beginner Zentangle Sunday, March 15 | 1–3 PM Zentangle: Beyond the Basics Sunday, March 22 | 1–3 PM Location: AW Gallery Instructor: Nancy Longman Cost: $35 per class (materials included) Class Size: Minimum 5 / Maximum 16 students Discover the relaxing and creative Zentangle Method, an easy-to-learn approach to creating beautiful, structured patterns. In the Beginner Zentangle class, students will learn the eight original steps of the Zentangle Method, foundational tangles (patterns), and basic principles and vocabulary. Working on small paper tiles, you’ll create your own original piece while enjoying a calming, meditative drawing experience. No experience necessary. All materials are provided, and a beginner toolkit is yours to keep. In Zentangle: Beyond the Basics, students who have completed a beginner class will build on their skills by exploring shading techniques using graphite to add depth, contrast, and dimension. You’ll learn new tangles, experiment with different “strings,” and discover finishing touches that bring your artwork to life. Materials are provided; students with a beginner toolkit are encouraged to bring it. Register: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Teen Volunteering
events.description:
Registration 

Join us for our monthly teen volunteer sessions. Keep an eye on our social media for dates. 

These sessions are designed for teens ONLY - 12 to 18 year olds are welcome. If you are over 18 years and are looking for a volunteer opportunity, please email emily@natickfarm.org . 

Volunteer work at NCOF is primarily farm chore-based and outdoors. So whether you’re looking to be in a beautiful setting, or enjoy meeting new people, or want to learn the principles behind organic farming, come volunteer on the farm.

You must be pre-registered to volunteer at NCOF and must complete the Volunteer Registration Form which includes our volunteer agreement and waiver. 

A donation of $10 per person is suggested to help offset the costs associated with coordinating and planning these weekend volunteer sessions:  Donate here

events.title: Waterfowl Workshop
events.description:
Registration 

$35/person
Whether you’ve had waterfowl for years or you’re still on the fence on whether to get them yourself, this class is for you! This early summer workshop will be run by Ryder, poultry enthusiast, and Meeghan, the livestock manager of the NCOF.

From their adorable quacking to their fun little waddles, waterfowl are easy to fall in love with. A large part of keeping waterfowl is learning from experience, which is why talking to someone who has had waterfowl can be so beneficial because it allows you to feel so much more confident. And the more confident that you are, the more fun you’ll end up having.

Although we will have a basic roadmap of what we want to talk about, your questions will ultimately dictate where we end up. This workshop is intended for ages 16 and up; if you have someone younger who is interested in attending, please reach out.

events.title: Kids Art and Leadership Lab Ages 11-13
events.description: Title: Kids’ Art & Leadership Lab: From the Inside Out Ages: 11–13 Dates: Saturdays – Jan 31, Feb 7, Feb 28, Mar 7, Mar 14, Mar 21, Mar 28, Apr 4 Time: 10:30 AM – 12:00 PM Tuition: $550 Materials Fee: $25 Minimum Enrollment: 8–15 students Location: Arts Wayland Register: www.artswayland.com Description: What if your child could learn to see their life the way an artist does — with imagination, openness, and courage to experiment? Designed for ages 11–13, this 8-week creative leadership lab blends art-making, brain science, and social-emotional learning to nurture focus, empathy, and resilience. Students explore how the principles of art—perspective, patience, expression—mirror the skills that shape emotional intelligence and leadership. Projects include designing meaningful keepsakes, guided reflection, and studying how creative leaders like Picasso and Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. turned vision into change. Each student builds toward a personal “masterpiece” of artistic and self-expression. Parents receive weekly recaps and simple at-home practices to extend learning beyond the classroom.

events.title: The Wee Farmers
events.description:
Registration 

Join us on the Farm each month for our drop-in Preschool Farm Series, designed for children ages 2.5-6. $25/person

Each session includes seasonal hands-on nature exploration, active movement and play, and a story, all with a knowledgeable, engaging farm educator.

All children must be pre-registered for these sessions. Only children need to be registered - adults do not need a ticket. 

If you have any questions, please email emily@natickfarm.org 

Have a preschool or friend group interested in booking a private program? We have various dates and times available, please email emily@natickfarm.org for more information.

events.title: The Artist Page
events.description: The Artist Page: A Creative Flow & Expression — A 6 Week Journey Creativity doesn’t come from trying harder — it comes from listening, softening, and allowing your voice to return. Join Isabel Zhang for The Artist Page, a six week guided creative practice designed to move beyond overthinking and into authentic expression. Each session includes grounding, gentle prompts for writing, drawing, or a blend of both, and optional sharing in a supportive, non evaluative space. Classes meet on Tuesdays from 10 to 11:30 AM on March 17, 24, 31 and April 7, 14, 28 (no session April 21) at Arts Wayland Gallery, 35 Andrew Ave, Wayland. Reclaim your creative flow and explore authentic expression — register now at www.artswayland.com .

events.title: 2026 Vision Board Workshop
events.description: Reset Your Nervous System: An Adult Creative Journey Into 2026 Arts Wayland Gallery | 4 Thursdays beginning January 8 10:30–11:45 AM Designed for adults, this 4-week creative series offers a grounding, neuroscience-based approach to closing out 2025 and stepping intentionally into 2026. More than an art class or vision board workshop, this is a hands-on experience that helps you regulate your nervous system, reduce stress, and rewire your inner state through non-verbal creative expression. Using rhythm, bilateral movement, gratitude practices, and guided reflection, participants build a 2026 vision from a calm, aligned, and empowered place. No art experience required — just curiosity and a willingness to play. Register at: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Mindfulness and Art Workshop
events.description: Mindfulness & Art: Brain Rewired is a 6-week experiential series designed to cultivate presence, emotional regulation, and creative resilience. Led by instructor Isabel Zhang at Arts Wayland, this unique program invites participants to explore mindfulness through guided meditation, reflective prompts, and neurographic art—an intuitive, calming art practice that gently engages the brain and nervous system. No prior art experience is needed. Each session offers a supportive space to stay present with discomfort, build flexibility and self-trust, and move forward with greater intention—both on the page and in daily life. Thursdays, March 5–April 9 | 10:30–12:00 PM Arts Wayland Gallery, Wayland $333 for the 6-week series Register at www.artswayland.com

The Wayland Depot, located in the historic train station in the center of Wayland, has hostess gifts, baby gifts, beautiful scarves and jewelry, local books, honey and maple syrup, plus Wayland Booster apparel. Stop in for that elusive find. Wednesday through Saturday, 10am – 4pm One Cochituate Road (rte. 27), Wayland, MA 01778 thewaylanddepot.com 508-358-5386

events.title: Kids Art and Leadership Lab Ages 11-13
events.description: Title: Kids’ Art & Leadership Lab: From the Inside Out Ages: 11–13 Dates: Saturdays – Jan 31, Feb 7, Feb 28, Mar 7, Mar 14, Mar 21, Mar 28, Apr 4 Time: 10:30 AM – 12:00 PM Tuition: $550 Materials Fee: $25 Minimum Enrollment: 8–15 students Location: Arts Wayland Register: www.artswayland.com Description: What if your child could learn to see their life the way an artist does — with imagination, openness, and courage to experiment? Designed for ages 11–13, this 8-week creative leadership lab blends art-making, brain science, and social-emotional learning to nurture focus, empathy, and resilience. Students explore how the principles of art—perspective, patience, expression—mirror the skills that shape emotional intelligence and leadership. Projects include designing meaningful keepsakes, guided reflection, and studying how creative leaders like Picasso and Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. turned vision into change. Each student builds toward a personal “masterpiece” of artistic and self-expression. Parents receive weekly recaps and simple at-home practices to extend learning beyond the classroom.

The March Member Exhibit
events.description: Arts Wayland Gallery: Members Exhibit Now through March 28th The Arts Wayland Gallery presents its month-long Members Exhibit, featuring one to five artworks from each participating member artist. This showcase includes a dynamic mix of paintings, photography, sculpture, and mixed‑media pieces, offering a rich view into the diverse creative talents within the Arts Wayland community. The exhibit is available both in person at the gallery and online at www.artswayland.com, where visitors can explore and purchase works directly from local artists. 📍 Location: Arts Wayland Gallery (and online) 🌐 Website: www.artswayland.com 🗓 Exhibit Dates: Through March 28th 💬 Admission: Free and open to the public

events.title: Zentangle Workshops
events.description: Zentangle with Nancy Longman: Beginner & Beyond the Basics Beginner Zentangle Sunday, March 15 | 1–3 PM Zentangle: Beyond the Basics Sunday, March 22 | 1–3 PM Location: AW Gallery Instructor: Nancy Longman Cost: $35 per class (materials included) Class Size: Minimum 5 / Maximum 16 students Discover the relaxing and creative Zentangle Method, an easy-to-learn approach to creating beautiful, structured patterns. In the Beginner Zentangle class, students will learn the eight original steps of the Zentangle Method, foundational tangles (patterns), and basic principles and vocabulary. Working on small paper tiles, you’ll create your own original piece while enjoying a calming, meditative drawing experience. No experience necessary. All materials are provided, and a beginner toolkit is yours to keep. In Zentangle: Beyond the Basics, students who have completed a beginner class will build on their skills by exploring shading techniques using graphite to add depth, contrast, and dimension. You’ll learn new tangles, experiment with different “strings,” and discover finishing touches that bring your artwork to life. Materials are provided; students with a beginner toolkit are encouraged to bring it. Register: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Echoes of Liberty Exhibit
events.description: Echoes of Liberty, a collaborative exhibition between Arts Wayland and the Wayland Historical Society & Museum, opens in May in honor of America’s 250th anniversary. Artists were invited to create works inspired by ten historical artifacts connected to themes of liberty, freedom, and the pursuit of happiness. A public reception will be held on May 9 from 3–5 PM at the Arts Wayland Gallery (35 Andrew Ave, Wayland), where guest juror Christie Jackson, Curator and Director of Exhibitions at the Concord Museum, will select and announce ten artworks for display at the Museum during June and July. The exhibit may be viewed online at https://artswayland.com/collections/may-2026-art-exhibit-eol or in person at the gallery.

events.title: High School Summer Program at Babson – Apply Now
events.description: Babson College is now accepting applications for the Arthur M. Blank School Summer Program, a pre-college experience for rising high school juniors and seniors (ages 16+). Students can choose from 50+ hands-on courses in areas like business, finance, AI, entrepreneurship, and more—offered on campus, online, or commuter. The program runs July 6–31, 2026. Space is limited, and courses fill on a rolling, first-come, first-served basis. Learn more, apply, or register for an upcoming info session: Link: https://www.babson.edu/summer-at-babson/high-school-learners/the-arthur-m-blank-school-summer-program/?utm_source=xchannel&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=CM_0_custom_260215_product_0___AWA_&utm_content=_click

events.title: The Artist Page
events.description: The Artist Page: A Creative Flow & Expression — A 6 Week Journey Creativity doesn’t come from trying harder — it comes from listening, softening, and allowing your voice to return. Join Isabel Zhang for The Artist Page, a six week guided creative practice designed to move beyond overthinking and into authentic expression. Each session includes grounding, gentle prompts for writing, drawing, or a blend of both, and optional sharing in a supportive, non evaluative space. Classes meet on Tuesdays from 10 to 11:30 AM on March 17, 24, 31 and April 7, 14, 28 (no session April 21) at Arts Wayland Gallery, 35 Andrew Ave, Wayland. Reclaim your creative flow and explore authentic expression — register now at www.artswayland.com .

Watercolor and Oil Pastel Workshop
events.description: Join us for an inspiring Watercolor Studio Class at the Arts Wayland Gallery (35 Andrew Ave)! Wednesdays, 5:45–7:15 PM • 8 Weeks • Starting January 7 Develop or refine your drawing and painting skills in this supportive, small-group studio class. Under the guidance of talented artist Rahul Ray, students will learn to draw objects three-dimensionally and bring them to life with watercolor. Great for both beginners and experienced artists—each participant is encouraged to grow their own unique style. Register now at www.artswayland.com

events.title: 2026 Vision Board Workshop
events.description: Reset Your Nervous System: An Adult Creative Journey Into 2026 Arts Wayland Gallery | 4 Thursdays beginning January 8 10:30–11:45 AM Designed for adults, this 4-week creative series offers a grounding, neuroscience-based approach to closing out 2025 and stepping intentionally into 2026. More than an art class or vision board workshop, this is a hands-on experience that helps you regulate your nervous system, reduce stress, and rewire your inner state through non-verbal creative expression. Using rhythm, bilateral movement, gratitude practices, and guided reflection, participants build a 2026 vision from a calm, aligned, and empowered place. No art experience required — just curiosity and a willingness to play. Register at: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Mindfulness and Art Workshop
events.description: Mindfulness & Art: Brain Rewired is a 6-week experiential series designed to cultivate presence, emotional regulation, and creative resilience. Led by instructor Isabel Zhang at Arts Wayland, this unique program invites participants to explore mindfulness through guided meditation, reflective prompts, and neurographic art—an intuitive, calming art practice that gently engages the brain and nervous system. No prior art experience is needed. Each session offers a supportive space to stay present with discomfort, build flexibility and self-trust, and move forward with greater intention—both on the page and in daily life. Thursdays, March 5–April 9 | 10:30–12:00 PM Arts Wayland Gallery, Wayland $333 for the 6-week series Register at www.artswayland.com

events.title: Rules of the Ride—bike handling and confidence for 5th to 8th graders
events.description: Rules of the Ride is a fun, hands-on way to learn bike handling and confidence for 5th to 8th graders. This year’s event is Sunday, June 7 from 10 a.m. to 11:30 a.m. at the Wellesley High School parking lot. Participants will learn how to ride safely and how to legally use critical skills training from MassBike instructors. The event includes a guided ride led by the Wellesley Police Department. During the event, helmets and bikes will be checked for proper fit and safety before moving into brief group instruction and the guided ride. Participants need a rideable bike, closed-toed shoes, and a helmet. Registration is required. (use Activity #231505).

2026 Vision Board Workshop
events.description: Reset Your Nervous System: An Adult Creative Journey Into 2026 Arts Wayland Gallery | 4 Thursdays beginning January 8 10:30–11:45 AM Designed for adults, this 4-week creative series offers a grounding, neuroscience-based approach to closing out 2025 and stepping intentionally into 2026. More than an art class or vision board workshop, this is a hands-on experience that helps you regulate your nervous system, reduce stress, and rewire your inner state through non-verbal creative expression. Using rhythm, bilateral movement, gratitude practices, and guided reflection, participants build a 2026 vision from a calm, aligned, and empowered place. No art experience required — just curiosity and a willingness to play. Register at: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Mindfulness and Art Workshop
events.description: Mindfulness & Art: Brain Rewired is a 6-week experiential series designed to cultivate presence, emotional regulation, and creative resilience. Led by instructor Isabel Zhang at Arts Wayland, this unique program invites participants to explore mindfulness through guided meditation, reflective prompts, and neurographic art—an intuitive, calming art practice that gently engages the brain and nervous system. No prior art experience is needed. Each session offers a supportive space to stay present with discomfort, build flexibility and self-trust, and move forward with greater intention—both on the page and in daily life. Thursdays, March 5–April 9 | 10:30–12:00 PM Arts Wayland Gallery, Wayland $333 for the 6-week series Register at www.artswayland.com

events.title: Zentangle Workshops
events.description: Zentangle with Nancy Longman: Beginner & Beyond the Basics Beginner Zentangle Sunday, March 15 | 1–3 PM Zentangle: Beyond the Basics Sunday, March 22 | 1–3 PM Location: AW Gallery Instructor: Nancy Longman Cost: $35 per class (materials included) Class Size: Minimum 5 / Maximum 16 students Discover the relaxing and creative Zentangle Method, an easy-to-learn approach to creating beautiful, structured patterns. In the Beginner Zentangle class, students will learn the eight original steps of the Zentangle Method, foundational tangles (patterns), and basic principles and vocabulary. Working on small paper tiles, you’ll create your own original piece while enjoying a calming, meditative drawing experience. No experience necessary. All materials are provided, and a beginner toolkit is yours to keep. In Zentangle: Beyond the Basics, students who have completed a beginner class will build on their skills by exploring shading techniques using graphite to add depth, contrast, and dimension. You’ll learn new tangles, experiment with different “strings,” and discover finishing touches that bring your artwork to life. Materials are provided; students with a beginner toolkit are encouraged to bring it. Register: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Kids Art and Leadership Lab Ages 11-13
events.description: Title: Kids’ Art & Leadership Lab: From the Inside Out Ages: 11–13 Dates: Saturdays – Jan 31, Feb 7, Feb 28, Mar 7, Mar 14, Mar 21, Mar 28, Apr 4 Time: 10:30 AM – 12:00 PM Tuition: $550 Materials Fee: $25 Minimum Enrollment: 8–15 students Location: Arts Wayland Register: www.artswayland.com Description: What if your child could learn to see their life the way an artist does — with imagination, openness, and courage to experiment? Designed for ages 11–13, this 8-week creative leadership lab blends art-making, brain science, and social-emotional learning to nurture focus, empathy, and resilience. Students explore how the principles of art—perspective, patience, expression—mirror the skills that shape emotional intelligence and leadership. Projects include designing meaningful keepsakes, guided reflection, and studying how creative leaders like Picasso and Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. turned vision into change. Each student builds toward a personal “masterpiece” of artistic and self-expression. Parents receive weekly recaps and simple at-home practices to extend learning beyond the classroom.

events.title: Zentangle Workshops
events.description: Zentangle with Nancy Longman: Beginner & Beyond the Basics Beginner Zentangle Sunday, March 15 | 1–3 PM Zentangle: Beyond the Basics Sunday, March 22 | 1–3 PM Location: AW Gallery Instructor: Nancy Longman Cost: $35 per class (materials included) Class Size: Minimum 5 / Maximum 16 students Discover the relaxing and creative Zentangle Method, an easy-to-learn approach to creating beautiful, structured patterns. In the Beginner Zentangle class, students will learn the eight original steps of the Zentangle Method, foundational tangles (patterns), and basic principles and vocabulary. Working on small paper tiles, you’ll create your own original piece while enjoying a calming, meditative drawing experience. No experience necessary. All materials are provided, and a beginner toolkit is yours to keep. In Zentangle: Beyond the Basics, students who have completed a beginner class will build on their skills by exploring shading techniques using graphite to add depth, contrast, and dimension. You’ll learn new tangles, experiment with different “strings,” and discover finishing touches that bring your artwork to life. Materials are provided; students with a beginner toolkit are encouraged to bring it. Register: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Kids Art and Leadership Lab Ages 11-13
events.description: Title: Kids’ Art & Leadership Lab: From the Inside Out Ages: 11–13 Dates: Saturdays – Jan 31, Feb 7, Feb 28, Mar 7, Mar 14, Mar 21, Mar 28, Apr 4 Time: 10:30 AM – 12:00 PM Tuition: $550 Materials Fee: $25 Minimum Enrollment: 8–15 students Location: Arts Wayland Register: www.artswayland.com Description: What if your child could learn to see their life the way an artist does — with imagination, openness, and courage to experiment? Designed for ages 11–13, this 8-week creative leadership lab blends art-making, brain science, and social-emotional learning to nurture focus, empathy, and resilience. Students explore how the principles of art—perspective, patience, expression—mirror the skills that shape emotional intelligence and leadership. Projects include designing meaningful keepsakes, guided reflection, and studying how creative leaders like Picasso and Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. turned vision into change. Each student builds toward a personal “masterpiece” of artistic and self-expression. Parents receive weekly recaps and simple at-home practices to extend learning beyond the classroom.

events.title: Art & the Family Archive | Collage as Protest and Portraiture with Ileana Doble Hernandez
events.description: Framingham-based artist Ileana Doble Hernandez invites you to reflect on the use of collage as a way to resist and explore our current socio-political situation, as well as a form of personal portrayal. Youll have time to create a small collage piece! We’ll provide blank paper, scissors, and magazines for clipping. You’re also welcome to bring your own magazines. Other materials like newspapers, discarded books, or copies of photographs also make great sources for collage.

events.title: The Artist Page
events.description: The Artist Page: A Creative Flow & Expression — A 6 Week Journey Creativity doesn’t come from trying harder — it comes from listening, softening, and allowing your voice to return. Join Isabel Zhang for The Artist Page, a six week guided creative practice designed to move beyond overthinking and into authentic expression. Each session includes grounding, gentle prompts for writing, drawing, or a blend of both, and optional sharing in a supportive, non evaluative space. Classes meet on Tuesdays from 10 to 11:30 AM on March 17, 24, 31 and April 7, 14, 28 (no session April 21) at Arts Wayland Gallery, 35 Andrew Ave, Wayland. Reclaim your creative flow and explore authentic expression — register now at www.artswayland.com .

events.description: Join us for an inspiring Watercolor Studio Class at the Arts Wayland Gallery (35 Andrew Ave)! Wednesdays, 5:45–7:15 PM • 8 Weeks • Starting January 7 Develop or refine your drawing and painting skills in this supportive, small-group studio class. events.title: 2026 Vision Board Workshop
events.description: Reset Your Nervous System: An Adult Creative Journey Into 2026 Arts Wayland Gallery | 4 Thursdays beginning January 8 10:30–11:45 AM Designed for adults, this 4-week creative series offers a grounding, neuroscience-based approach to closing out 2025 and stepping intentionally into 2026. More than an art class or vision board workshop, this is a hands-on experience that helps you regulate your nervous system, reduce stress, and rewire your inner state through non-verbal creative expression. Using rhythm, bilateral movement, gratitude practices, and guided reflection, participants build a 2026 vision from a calm, aligned, and empowered place. No art experience required — just curiosity and a willingness to play. Register at: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Mindfulness and Art Workshop
events.description: Mindfulness & Art: Brain Rewired is a 6-week experiential series designed to cultivate presence, emotional regulation, and creative resilience. Led by instructor Isabel Zhang at Arts Wayland, this unique program invites participants to explore mindfulness through guided meditation, reflective prompts, and neurographic art—an intuitive, calming art practice that gently engages the brain and nervous system. No prior art experience is needed. Each session offers a supportive space to stay present with discomfort, build flexibility and self-trust, and move forward with greater intention—both on the page and in daily life. Thursdays, March 5–April 9 | 10:30–12:00 PM Arts Wayland Gallery, Wayland $333 for the 6-week series Register at www.artswayland.com

events.title: 2026 Vision Board Workshop
events.description: Reset Your Nervous System: An Adult Creative Journey Into 2026 Arts Wayland Gallery | 4 Thursdays beginning January 8 10:30–11:45 AM Designed for adults, this 4-week creative series offers a grounding, neuroscience-based approach to closing out 2025 and stepping intentionally into 2026. More than an art class or vision board workshop, this is a hands-on experience that helps you regulate your nervous system, reduce stress, and rewire your inner state through non-verbal creative expression. Using rhythm, bilateral movement, gratitude practices, and guided reflection, participants build a 2026 vision from a calm, aligned, and empowered place. No art experience required — just curiosity and a willingness to play. Register at: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Mindfulness and Art Workshop
events.description: Mindfulness & Art: Brain Rewired is a 6-week experiential series designed to cultivate presence, emotional regulation, and creative resilience. Led by instructor Isabel Zhang at Arts Wayland, this unique program invites participants to explore mindfulness through guided meditation, reflective prompts, and neurographic art—an intuitive, calming art practice that gently engages the brain and nervous system. No prior art experience is needed. Each session offers a supportive space to stay present with discomfort, build flexibility and self-trust, and move forward with greater intention—both on the page and in daily life. Thursdays, March 5–April 9 | 10:30–12:00 PM Arts Wayland Gallery, Wayland $333 for the 6-week series Register at www.artswayland.com

events.title: Zentangle Workshops
events.description: Zentangle with Nancy Longman: Beginner & Beyond the Basics Beginner Zentangle Sunday, March 15 | 1–3 PM Zentangle: Beyond the Basics Sunday, March 22 | 1–3 PM Location: AW Gallery Instructor: Nancy Longman Cost: $35 per class (materials included) Class Size: Minimum 5 / Maximum 16 students Discover the relaxing and creative Zentangle Method, an easy-to-learn approach to creating beautiful, structured patterns. In the Beginner Zentangle class, students will learn the eight original steps of the Zentangle Method, foundational tangles (patterns), and basic principles and vocabulary. Working on small paper tiles, you’ll create your own original piece while enjoying a calming, meditative drawing experience. No experience necessary. All materials are provided, and a beginner toolkit is yours to keep. In Zentangle: Beyond the Basics, students who have completed a beginner class will build on their skills by exploring shading techniques using graphite to add depth, contrast, and dimension. You’ll learn new tangles, experiment with different “strings,” and discover finishing touches that bring your artwork to life. Materials are provided; students with a beginner toolkit are encouraged to bring it. Register: www.artswayland.com

events.title: Kids Art and Leadership Lab Ages 11-13
events.description: Title: Kids’ Art & Leadership Lab: From the Inside Out Ages: 11–13 Dates: Saturdays – Jan 31, Feb 7, Feb 28, Mar 7, Mar 14, Mar 21, Mar 28, Apr 4 Time: 10:30 AM – 12:00 PM Tuition: $550 Materials Fee: $25 Minimum Enrollment: 8–15 students Location: Arts Wayland Register: www.artswayland.com Description: What if your child could learn to see their life the way an artist does — with imagination, openness, and courage to experiment? Designed for ages 11–13, this 8-week creative leadership lab blends art-making, brain science, and social-emotional learning to nurture focus, empathy, and resilience. Students explore how the principles of art—perspective, patience, expression—mirror the skills that shape emotional intelligence and leadership. Projects include designing meaningful keepsakes, guided reflection, and studying how creative leaders like Picasso and Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. turned vision into change. Each student builds toward a personal “masterpiece” of artistic and self-expression. Parents receive weekly recaps and simple at-home practices to extend learning beyond the classroom.

events.title: Kids Art and Leadership Lab Ages 11-13
events.description: Title: Kids’ Art & Leadership Lab: From the Inside Out Ages: 11–13 Dates: Saturdays – Jan 31, Feb 7, Feb 28, Mar 7, Mar 14, Mar 21, Mar 28, Apr 4 Time: 10:30 AM – 12:00 PM Tuition: $550 Materials Fee: $25 Minimum Enrollment: 8–15 students Location: Arts Wayland Register: www.artswayland.com Description: What if your child could learn to see their life the way an artist does — with imagination, openness, and courage to experiment? Designed for ages 11–13, this 8-week creative leadership lab blends art-making, brain science, and social-emotional learning to nurture focus, empathy, and resilience. Students explore how the principles of art—perspective, patience, expression—mirror the skills that shape emotional intelligence and leadership. Projects include designing meaningful keepsakes, guided reflection, and studying how creative leaders like Picasso and Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. turned vision into change. Each student builds toward a personal “masterpiece” of artistic and self-expression. Parents receive weekly recaps and simple at-home practices to extend learning beyond the classroom.

events.title: The Artist Page
events.description: The Artist Page: A Creative Flow & Expression — A 6 Week Journey Creativity doesn’t come from trying harder — it comes from listening, softening, and allowing your voice to return. Join Isabel Zhang for The Artist Page, a six week guided creative practice designed to move beyond overthinking and into authentic expression. Each session includes grounding, gentle prompts for writing, drawing, or a blend of both, and optional sharing in a supportive, non evaluative space. Classes meet on Tuesdays from 10 to 11:30 AM on March 17, 24, 31 and April 7, 14, 28 (no session April 21) at Arts Wayland Gallery, 35 Andrew Ave, Wayland. Reclaim your creative flow and explore authentic expression — register now at www.artswayland.com .
